1 hour ago

--

Over the past month, I had the incredible opportunity to work as a Web Development intern at Oasis Infobyte (OIB-SIP) (oasisinfobyte.com). This virtual internship was designed to bridge the gap between classroom theory and practical, real-world application. During this cohort, I successfully completed three distinct tasks that helped sharpen my foundational frontend skills.
Here is a breakdown of the projects I developed:
Task 1: Landing Page
For my first task, I built a responsive landing page. The goal was to focus on layout structure, clean UI/UX, and call-to-action placement. I utilized semantic HTML elements to ensure the page was well-structured and highly accessible.
Task 2: Personal Portfolio
The second task was creating a personal portfolio website. This project allowed me to showcase my skills, academic background, and projects in one centralized place. It served as a fantastic exercise in self-branding and structuring a professional web layout.
Task 3: Temperature Converter Website
The final task was a functional temperature converter. This project required building a clean user interface where users can seamlessly convert temperatures between Celsius, Fahrenheit, and Kelvin. It was a great way to handle user inputs and layout alignment.
Key Takeaways
This internship gave me valuable experience in:

• Writing structured, clean HTML,CSS, Javascript layout logic.

• Managing project source code and learning the workflows of version control with GitHub.

• Working independently and delivering projects within a given timeline.
I am incredibly grateful to the team at Oasis Infobyte for providing this structured learning platform. It has given me a major boost in confidence as I continue my journey in software engineering!
#webdevelopment #oasisinfobyte #internship #tech
